faqs.question: What types of ticks are common in Hackensack, NJ?
faqs.answer: Hackensack residents commonly encounter deer ticks (blacklegged ticks), American dog ticks, and lone star ticks. Deer ticks are of particular concern as they can transmit Lyme disease, which is prevalent in Bergen County. Our tick control treatments target all species and their nymphs to provide comprehensive protection.

faqs.question: How much does tick control cost in Hackensack?
faqs.answer: Our tick control services in Hackensack range from $125 to $325, depending on property size, infestation severity, and treatment frequency. We offer one-time applications, seasonal programs, and year-round protection plans. A free inspection allows us to provide an accurate quote tailored to your specific needs.

faqs.question: When is tick season in Hackensack, NJ?
faqs.answer: Tick season in Hackensack typically runs from April through November, with peak activity in May through August. However, ticks can be active during mild winter days when temperatures exceed 45°F. We recommend starting preventive treatments in early spring and maintaining protection through fall for optimal results.

faqs.question: Are tick control treatments safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-registered products specifically designed for residential tick control that are safe when applied by our licensed professionals. Treatments dry within 1-2 hours, after which your yard is safe for children and pets. We also offer organic and natural tick control options for clients preferring eco-friendly solutions.
